source_text: Session 3 - Bringing Your AI Into the World

records.title: Bringing Your AI Into the World
records.subtitle: Customize your AI with connectors and skills, then build your prototype.

source_text: Building and testing the AI Prototype
Build a reusable AI prototype aligned to the workflow
Test the prototype against real examples
Compare outputs to defined success criteria and refine for reliability

left.label: Focus
left.body: Building and testing the AI Prototype

right.label: Goals
bullets: Build a reusable AI prototype aligned to the workflow
Apply structured prompting and context-setting
Test the prototype against real examples
Compare outputs to defined success criteria and refine for reliability

source_text: Stand up, find someone you don’t know, and share the AI project you’re working on.
What’s one new thing you learned about your workflows since our last session?

source_text: Answer these questions before you deploy and you’re ahead of 99% of social sector tech.
How will we know if it’s failing?
What does success look like - for everyone?
Who can raise an issue, and what happens?
Who can change it or turn it off?

records.title: Answer these before you deploy
records.intro: Answer these questions before you deploy and you’re ahead of 99% of social sector tech.
bullets: What does success look like - for everyone?
How will we know if it’s failing?
Who can raise an issue, and what happens?
Who can change it or turn it off?

source_text: These are tough! There’s no right answer, just what your organization cares about more.

records.title: These are tough — there’s no right answer

left.label: On one side
bullets: Speed
Serving more people
Staff time saved
Consistency

right.label: On the other
bullets: Accuracy
Serving fewer people better
User experience
Flexibility

records.note: These are tough! There’s no right answer, just what your organization cares about more.

source_text: For-profits rarely let customers change the product. But you serve your community. You have flexibility they don't!

records.title: What if your constituents could…
records.intro: For-profits rarely let customers change the product. But you serve your community. You have flexibility they don't!
bullets: Opt out?
Raise an issue?
Override the AI?

records.title: …but you have control over that!
records.body: Tool permissions let you choose when Claude is allowed to use each tool — always allow, ask each time, or require approval.

source_text: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
Skills are actions your Claude can take any time you or anyone calls it.
Jenni’s case study publishing Claude project

steps.label: Jenni’s case study publishing Claude project

records.note: Three separate Claude projects all converge on one shared skill. Rebranded the diagram's 'Decoded Futures branding skill' box to 'ImpactAI Foundry branding skill'.

source_text: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
Why use skills? From Anthropic themselves:
Improvement in Claude’s performance of specific tasks

records.title: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
records.intro: Why use skills? From Anthropic themselves:
bullets: Improvement in Claude’s performance of specific tasks
Organizational knowledge capture
Easy customization
Centralized management for organizations

records.caption: The Skills settings panel: a 'brand-guidelines' skill (Trigger: slash command + auto) applies official brand colors and typography to any artifact.
records.note: Full-bleed product screenshot of the Claude Skills customization UI showing the brand-guidelines skill.

source_text: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
But how do I make new skills?
Great news, Anthropic thought of that: you just ask!
Claude has a skill-creator skill! (so meta)

records.title: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
records.body:
But how do I make new skills?

Great news, Anthropic thought of that: you just ask!

Claude has a skill-creator skill! (so meta)

source_text: Create a brand-guidelines skill for your actual brand.

records.title: Create a brand-guidelines skill for your actual brand.

rows.body: “Create me a brand guidelines skill that is based off of the real brand guidelines of my foundation”

source_text: Skills: Teaching Claude Some New Tricks
Useful for small, contained workflows that you and your organization would use a lot
Smaller than a project - you could imagine a project to write social media communications using your brand guidelines tool.
Called with either a “/” or by Claude’s choice.

records.title: Skills Summary
bullets: Useful for small, contained workflows that you and your organization would use a lot
Smaller than a project - you could imagine a project to write social media communications using your brand guidelines tool.
Called with either a “/” or by Claude’s choice.

source_text: Any problem that can be solved by computing is arguably “solvable” by LLMs.
Prior to language models: People had to “speak computer” to build software.
Now: Computers understand how people speak, and will build software as described.

records.title: Any problem that can be solved by computing is arguably “solvable” by LLMs.

left.label: Prior to language models
left.body: People had to “speak computer” to build software.

right.label: Now
right.body: Computers understand how people speak, and will build software as described.

source_text: …but don’t let these get in the way of getting started
Keep a Human in the Loop
Free AI tools aren’t private - don’t share information unless it’s a secure platform.
Until you fully understand how AI behaves, always review its outputs before relying on them.
Out-of-the-box AI models don’t reflect every culture or experience - adapt them to truly serve your audience.

records.title: Ethics Guidelines
records.kicker: …but don't let these get in the way of getting started

rows.body: Free AI tools aren't private - don't share information unless it's a secure platform.

rows.lead: Keep a Human in the Loop
rows.body: Until you fully understand how AI behaves, always review its outputs before relying on them.
Out-of-the-box AI models don't reflect every culture or experience - adapt them to truly serve your audience.
"You are a [add an expert, such as a "social media expert"] and your objective is to [add goal, such as "create LinkedIn posts about events"]"
"To complete these instructions, follow these steps: Step 1, Step 2, Step 3 (etc)"
"When you are done, write your final response": a summary paragraph of the work done, and 3 bullet points, one per next step

source_text: “I get a lot of questions and can’t keep up with answering them all.”
“I keep doing the same tasks over and over and it’s taking time away from more important work.”
“I need to write or create something to share with others.”
“I spend too much time reviewing information that follows a pattern.”
“I want to learn something new or need better insight to make smarter choices.”

rows.body: "I get a lot of questions and can't keep up with answering them all."
"I keep doing the same tasks over and over and it's taking time away from more important work."
"I need to write or create something to share with others."
"I spend too much time reviewing information that follows a pattern."
"I want to learn something new or need better insight to make smarter choices."

source_text: WE LEARNED TO MAKE TOAST (aka WORKFLOWS)

records.title: We learned to make toast (aka workflows)
records.body: A workflow is a chain of steps. Each box in the chain is a node.

source_text: For any single step in a workflow, you can break it down into:
The result of the step and how you know it’s successful
The signal to start the step
The actions the actor does to complete the step
Info or data needed to complete the step (this is optional!)
The person or thing that completes the step

records.title: Zoom in on one step (node)

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: The signal to start the step

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The person or thing that completes the step

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: Info or data needed to complete the step (this is optional!)

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: The actions the actor does to complete the step

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The result of the step and how you know it's successful

records.caption: For any single step in a workflow, you can break it down into:

records.eyebrow: What Teams Go On To Build
records.title: What teams go on to build
records.intro: Outcomes from the program model ImpactAI Foundry is built on:
bullets: Build Policy — an AI policy for a 600-person organization
Create Apps — an app to support nonprofits nationwide
Lead Learning — an AI curriculum for a statewide network of Entrepreneurs Assistance Centers
Get Funded — $500,000 in follow-on funding for AI tools
Get Published — published on training the workforce for an AI era
Scale — using AI to scale programming nationally
records.note: These are illustrative outcomes from the program model (IAF is cohort one). Swap for IAF's own alumni results once available.
